Cardiovascular

Resting HR daily readings + 7-day rolling avg · 30d

Daily 7-day avg
Daily RHR is noisy (sleep, hydration, alcohol can swing it ±5 bpm). The rolling average is the trend that matters; downward = improving cardiovascular efficiency.

Blood pressure sys / dia · 30d

Target: <120 / <80 (AHA normal). Both tracking in-range.

HRV ms · overnight · recovery quality

Higher = better recovery. Watch for dips after heavy-volume weeks.

Daily steps 30d · target 10,000

Grouped by primary muscle focus (the highest-volume muscle group per session).

Recovery Signals daily composite

Composite of sleep score, HRV, and resting HR z-scores — normalized against your own 30-day window (so the average trends toward 50 by design; the chart shows relative recovery swings).
≥ 70 — strong recovery 50–69 — average < 50 — below your own baseline

Hormone Panel

Testosterone — Total & Free ng/dL · over time

Total T (ng/dL) Free T (ng/dL ×10) Optimal range

Estradiol (E2) & SHBG pg/mL · ng/dL

Estradiol (pg/mL) SHBG (nmol/L)
E2 rises with TRT — watch for elevation above 40 pg/mL. SHBG affects free T availability.

Hematocrit & Hemoglobin % · g/dL ×3

TRT raises hematocrit. Flag if >54% — may require therapeutic phlebotomy.
